ops.title: Watcher gate scores through the served MLX artifact (eval-what-you-serve)
ops.body:
The watcher promotion gate (`cc_steer/retrain/watcher.py`) scores the frozen eval frame through the ACTUAL served artifact — the converted mlx-lm adapter on `mlx-community/Qwen3-8B-4bit`, f32-normalized softmax — NOT via Tinker `compute_logprobs` on the full-precision `Qwen/Qwen3-8B` frame it trained against. Reason (T3.1): 4-bit quantization shifts mid-confidence P(NO_STEER), so the Tinker-side metric described a model we do not serve — measured max abs diff 0.26 on the first clean-data retrain, which the old MLX parity gate then rejected as its own drift.

The fresh-epoch AUC floor, `corrected_gate`, the served threshold, and `write_probs` all consume the LOCAL served probs (`_score_frame_local`), so future incumbent-vs-candidate comparisons run channel-consistent (local vs local). Tinker still trains + picks the best checkpoint by val AUC + enforces the spend cap. The parity gate is DELETED; a non-blocking serving-drift diagnostic (`_serving_diagnostic`, behind the `_safe_serving_diagnostic` boundary) samples the frame Tinker-vs-served and persists per-row diffs to `serving_diagnostic.json` on every converted candidate — it never blocks (a conversion bug shows up as served AUC at chance, which the floor rejects).

Full-frame local scoring is memory-bounded via per-row `MlxDrafter.clear_cache()` (`mx.clear_cache`) — this is what makes it safe despite the E12 Jetsam kill (unbounded MLX buffer-cache growth). VALIDATED on the rejected run's converted artifact: 628 rows, served AUC 0.929, fresh-epoch floor cleared, 0.405 rows/s, RSS flat at 2.9GB the whole ~26min, no Jetsam. This supersedes the prior "keep eval on Tinker, never local" guidance — local scoring is now the gate channel, made safe by design. Recipe knobs `parity_rows`/`parity_tolerance` renamed `diagnostic_rows`/`diagnostic_tolerance` (diagnostic-only). Commits 41950b4f (f32 softmax), a2804814 (gate+diagnostic+recipe), 40516247 (diagnostic boundary).
